title: oxalis wrightii var. pilosa
intro: Oxalis wrightii var. pilosa, also known as the hairy wood-sorrel or hairy yellow woodsorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, making it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Oxalis wrightii var. pilosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, act as an antioxidant, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can contribute to its use in treating various health issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing its leaves and roots in hot water to create a tea, or by using it as a tincture to create a concentrated extract.
